category_title: Fourth-grade students who initially dislike reading but engage through self-selection
minimal_context: The student is sitting in a fourth-grade classroom at the beginning of the semester, being asked to read from the basal reading series.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for reading class. Please take out your basal reading series book and start reading the first story.

STUDENT: Do I have to read this? I don't want to.

TUTOR: Yes, everyone needs to read the assigned story right now.

STUDENT: Can I just fill out one of those slips for recommending books instead?

TUTOR: No, right now we are reading the basal reader. Please focus on the text.

STUDENT: I'm not going to read it. I hate reading and reading class.

options.A: Design a storyboard with detailed pictures of each scene from the basal reading series for the student to use during reading class.
options.B: Deliver twelve hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a structured mindfulness intervention program for the student to complete during the semester.
options.C: Avoid using the basal reading series and instead allow the student to self-select books that are appropriate for their reading level and that interest them.
options.D: Immediately discipline the student, reminding them that they are not allowed to talk and must raise their hand, enforcing strict silence during reading class.
